title: Chunk to preserve meaning
description: Documents are split on semantic boundaries with overlap so a chunk is a self-contained, retrievable unit of meaning.
body: Use when ingesting documents into the index. Do not split on a fixed character count that cuts sentences or separates a claim from its context, and avoid chunks so large they dilute the embedding. Actions: split on structural/semantic boundaries (headings, paragraphs), add overlap to preserve cross-boundary context, and size chunks to the embedding model's effective range. Validation signals include a retrieval test that a query whose answer spans a boundary still retrieves a chunk containing the full answer. Risk boundary: chunking that strips the context needed to interpret a passage causes ungrounded answers; treat it as a quality defect, not a tuning knob.
